rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF GEORGINA LAXMI LOREK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Authority To Create Trust, Transfer Funds, And Terminate Guardianship Estate; Authority To Pay Child Care And Camp Expenses From Guardianship Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) No income and expense declaration (FL-150 form) of the guardian has been filed since it was requested before the initial hearing on 06/27/2023 (see also tentative rulings for 07/25/2023 & 08/22/2023 hearings reiterating the request). Other than the social security survivor benefits and cash distribution from the deceased spouse's probate estate, the guardian has not disclosed any information about his financial circumstances for the Court to make a good cause determination required by Probate Code section 2422. 2) Section 3611(g), cited in the petition as statutory authority to create a minor's trust, only applies when there is no guardianship estate. See section 3610. Section 3602 (presumably subdivision(c)(3)), also cited in the petition as statutory authority to create a minor's trust, only allows the "court giving the judgment for a minor described in section 3600" to create or approve the trust, but the order for final distribution issued in the related probate case does not appear to qualify as the "judgment" defined in section 3600. See Estate of Kampen (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 971, 986-988 ("It is not a judgment that requires the payment of money. Rather, it is a judgment distributing assets. The distribution order settles all claims involving all the assets in the estate, and is clearly not a money judgment in favor of one particular party"). If the order for final distribution should be treated as the section 3600 judgment, the request to create a trust for each minor had to be made in the related probate case before the Court issued the final distribution order, based on the statutory language of section 3602(c)(3) ("court giving the judgment referred to in section 3600" and "trust which is either created by, or approved of, in the judgment described in section 3600"). The judgment (order for final distribution) has become final and conclusive, as it has not been appealed or set aside to add a provision creating or approving a distributee trust for each minor. Even if the belated request to create a minor's trust should be enteratined, the proposed trust does not appear to benefit either minor, in that: the minors cannot revoke or amend their trust until they turn 21 years of age; in the event of either minor's death, the guardian would be the sole beneficiary as the minor's sole heir; and if the trust is placed under the Court's supervision, the trustee is subject to the same terms and conditions as the guardian of the estate, and prior court approval is required for many acts of the trustee including distribution from the trust to pay the minors' expenses. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVA C. TOLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice for a petition for final distribution is the mandatory Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. (There is no reason to serve a proposed order; the hearing is of the petition.) (2) If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the complete Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to that proof. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) In any event, mailing notice on 10/4/23 was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (5) "Amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3(3).] This "First Amended Report," etc., should stand on its own and not reference a previously filed petition that has been superseded by this amended petition. (6) This amended petition does not even have exhibits I and J mentioned in paragraph Y attached to it. (7) The calculation of the statutory compensation appears to be incorrect. (8) If the declaration in support of the fees requested by the attorney for extraordinary services did not fully comply with CRC 7.702, it must. (After all issues are addressed, an appearance will likely be required. The administrator is the sole heir. The estate could have closed in October 2022) (9) Under what authority was Probate Advance LLP paid $317,000? Proof? (10) What child support payments and to whom? Deadline is 11/21/23 for providing the assigned examiner the proposed order and courtesy copies of the second (fully) amended petition and proof of service of notice of that second amended petition, showing service of notice after the second amended petition is e-filed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] There is no need to attach copies of prior filed petitions to a petition for final distribution. The administrator was appointed on 5/18/22, but he did not claim his Letters until 6/22/22. Probate, not Penal, Code Section 216)

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/5/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No will has been lodged. (2) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Deadline is 11/21/23 for providing the assigned examiner the proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A proposed order should be provided in hard copy to the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies were received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.])
